turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been experiencing some pretty concerning symptoms over the past three days. I've got this tightness in my chest that's like a pressure sensation.

turns.student: Can you tell me more about what you've been feeling?
turns.patient: It's been getting worse, to be honest - it started off mild but now it feels like someone is squeezing my chest.

turns.student: When you have those episodes, do you notice anything else happening to your body?
turns.patient: Yeah, I get a little short of breath when it happens.

turns.student: Any sweating or clamminess when the symptoms hit?
turns.patient: I did notice some sweating during those episodes, yeah.

turns.student: So no perspiration at all during the episodes?
turns.patient: Actually, yes. I mentioned earlier that I did get a little sweaty during those episodes. It was just mild - I don't know how to describe it, but it's definitely been there when the chest tightness is happening.

turns.student: Does the discomfort stay in one spot, or does it move around?
turns.patient: It's kind of centered in my chest, but sometimes it radiates a bit to the left side.

turns.student: Does it radiate anywhere — like your arms, neck, or jaw?
turns.patient: It radiates to my left arm.

turns.student: Any tingling or numbness in your arms when it happens?
turns.patient: I did notice some tingling in my left arm during the more severe episodes. It's hard to describe, but it feels like a pins-and-needles sensation.

turns.student: What about your extremities — any tingling, pain, or unusual sensations?
turns.patient: I've had some mild numbness in my feet lately, which is why I mentioned that my shoes feel tighter than usual. It's not too bad.

turns.student: Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Ankles, feet, hands?
turns.patient: Yeah, I have been noticing some mild bilateral ankle swelling. My shoes do feel a bit tighter than usual.

turns.student: Do your shoes feel tighter than usual, or any puffiness in your legs?
turns.patient: Yeah, my shoes feel tighter than usual and there's just a general feeling of puffiness in my ankles and lower legs.

turns.student: Any stomach issues during the episodes? Nausea, queasiness, upset stomach?
turns.patient: Actually, I get a little nauseous during those chest tightness episodes. It's not too bad, just a mild feeling of discomfort in my stomach.

turns.student: Do you feel sick to your stomach when the symptoms come on?
turns.patient: It's more like a mild discomfort or unease in my stomach, but I wouldn't say it's a full-blown feeling of sickness. It's hard to describe, but it's definitely there when the chest tightness is happening.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
turns.patient: No, no fevers or chills for me, just this chest tightness and shortness of breath.

turns.student: Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or weight?
turns.patient: No, my appetite's been fine, but I have gained a bit of weight recently.

turns.student: Tell me about your lifestyle — diet, exercise, daily routine.
turns.patient: I've got to say, I'm not exactly living the healthiest lifestyle. I'm a long-haul truck driver, so I spend most of my week on the road. When I'm home, I tend to grab fast food pretty often - it's just convenient, you know? As for exercise, let's just say I don't get as much physical activity as I should.

turns.student: How active are you physically? Do you work out or walk regularly?
turns.patient: I'm not very active at all. My job is pretty sedentary and even when I'm home, I don't really have time to exercise.

turns.student: What does a typical day of eating look like for you?
turns.patient: It's usually fast food or takeout, maybe some leftovers from last night if I'm lucky. I'd say 5-6 days out of the week is pretty normal for me.

turns.student: Any medical problems in your family? Parents, siblings?
turns.patient: My dad had a heart attack at age 52 and passed away. My mom has hypertension and type 2 diabetes, but she's still alive at 78.

turns.student: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious diseases?
turns.patient: My dad passed away from a heart attack when he was 52 years old.
